mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held at a low, stable level relative to its trailing 12-month average signals continued accommodative monetary policy, which supports risk asset valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns is below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive-return hit rate falls below 50% during periods of low stable rates.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE declines by more than 0.2 percentage points over a 3-month window, it signals accelerating labor market healing that raises household income expectations and consumer spending capacity, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns following triggered declines are not significantly higher than the unconditional baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or hit rate below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month acceleration in INDPRO and RSAFS indicates that both production and consumption are expanding in tandem, a joint confirmation of broadening economic momentum that is a stronger positive signal for equities than either series alone over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint acceleration signal fails to outperform single-series INDPRO-only or RSAFS-only models on rank IC by at least 0.02 over a 2-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-recession housing recovery regime, sustained month-over-month increases in HOUST reflect renewed confidence in durable-goods and construction spending, feeding through to broader corporate earnings expectations and positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: During periods of confirmed HOUST uptrend, forward 60-day SPX returns are negative in more than 45% of instances, or the effect disappears outside the identified recovery regime, indicating no regime dependence.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in INDPRO reflects expanding industrial output and near-term corporate revenue momentum, producing a short-horizon positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO month-over-month change and forward 5-day returns is below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent improvement in both PAYEMS (rising nonfarm payrolls) and UNRATE (falling unemployment rate) provides a confirmed dual-signal of labor market strength that is more predictive of positive equity returns over the next month than either indicator alone, since divergences between the two often reflect noisy or transitory data.
failure_condition: Joint PAYEMS-UNRATE improvement signal fails to beat either single-series baseline by rank IC margin of 0.02 over a rolling 2-year evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS rises above 4.5% and continues climbing, it signals a tightening monetary regime that raises the discount rate on future equity cash flows and increases borrowing costs, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and rising for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Forward 60-day SPX returns following triggered tightening episodes are positive in more than 55% of instances, invalidating the negative-direction claim.

mechanism_narrative: During an early-cycle expansion regime, strong month-over-month growth in RSAFS indicates robust consumer demand that quickly feeds into near-term retail and consumer discretionary earnings surprises, generating a short-horizon positive drift in equity prices over the next week.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS month-over-month growth and forward 5-day returns falls below 0.02 during early-cycle expansion periods over any rolling 2-year window.
